title: EMILIA PÉREZ
writer: Jacques Audiard in collaboration with Thomas Bidegain
logline: A dissatisfied defense attorney in Mexico City becomes entangled with a powerful cartel leader who undergoes gender transition surgery and later creates a foundation to find missing persons, leading to a deadly confrontation between past and present identities.
protagonist: Rita (defense attorney who becomes involved with Manitas/Emilia)
antagonistOrConflict: The violent world of Mexican cartels and the impossibility of truly escaping one's past
tone: Dark, operatic, and tragic with moments of hope and redemption

actBreaks.description: Emilia reveals her new identity to Rita in London, four years after the transition, launching the story into its second phase.

validation.ruling: The family packs to leave Switzerland and return to Mexico after four years, with Rita explaining Emilia as Manitas' distant cousin who will shelter them, fundamentally shifting the story from the surgery/transformation phase to the family reunion phase.

actBreaks.description: The violent confrontation erupts as Emilia's past catches up to her present life, with bodies discovered and Rita beaten.

validation.ruling: Gustavo Brun is brutally beaten and paid $100,000 to leave Mexico City permanently, representing the violent resolution of the romantic triangle and forcing the story into its final act of consequences and resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This musical crime drama follows a classical three-act structure but with an unconventional protagonist journey. Rita serves as our entry point into Manitas' world, but the story ultimately becomes about Emilia's transformation and the impossibility of escaping one's violent past. The structure cleverly uses a four-year time jump to show the consequences of choices, with the middle section focusing on Emilia's attempts at redemption through her missing persons foundation. The musical numbers punctuate emotional beats and character revelations rather than advancing plot, creating an operatic quality that elevates the material beyond standard crime drama.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The opening moves swiftly through Rita's dissatisfaction and recruitment (scenes 1-16), then slows for character development during the desert meetings and surgery preparations. The four-year jump at scene 44 creates a major pacing shift, introducing a more contemplative middle section focused on Emilia's foundation work. The final act accelerates dramatically from scene 83 onward, with rapid cuts between violence and confrontation leading to the tragic climax.

incitingIncident.description: Manitas tasks Rita with finding a surgeon for his gender transition surgery, offering unlimited resources.

validation.ruling: Rita receives unlimited resources and a mission to find a surgeon for Manitas' gender reassignment surgery, fundamentally changing her role from lawyer to facilitator of a dangerous cartel boss's transformation and establishing the central conflict.

midpoint.description: In a hangar with ten body bags, Emilia embraces a grieving woman from the cemetery and sings 'La Lucecita' about feeling love for herself for the first time when the woman kissed her hands.

validation.ruling: The candidate lacks the transformative moment typical of a midpoint. Scene 60 better serves as the midpoint where Emilia embraces a grieving mother in the hangar filled with body bags and sings 'La Lucecita,' marking her transformation from self-serving to genuinely caring for the families of the disappeared.

climax.description: A car zigzags while being shot at with detonations and muzzle flashes illuminating the interior, then swerves and flies down an embankment.

validation.ruling: The candidate is aftermath, not peak confrontation. Scene 101 is the true climax with the car chase under gunfire, zigzagging through detonations and muzzle flashes before crashing down the embankment - the highest tension moment of direct confrontation and mortal danger.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO CITY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mexico City skyline at night with distant vendor's chant echoing through the city.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT MENDOZA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A woman is violently attacked and thrown from a luxury apartment balcony to her death.

scenes.heading: INT. 7-ELEVEN MARKET - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rita practices her legal closing arguments alone in a 7-Eleven convenience store.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN CHAPULTEPEC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita walks through empty streets singing about going up and down, joined by a growing crowd.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN CHAPULTEPEC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita leads a crowd through a market singing about violence, justice, and corruption while images of the Mendozas appear.

scenes.heading: INT. MEXICO CITY COURTROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita watches her boss Berlinger deliver her closing argument in the Mendoza murder trial while she mouths along.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita calls her mother to report they won the case but expresses having a bad taste in her mouth.

scenes.heading: INT. WOMEN’S RESTROOM COURTHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita has blood on her hands in a courthouse restroom when an unknown caller contacts her, knowing her location.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita follows cleaning ladies through the courthouse singing about how long she'll keep her head down and work for nothing.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEWSSTAND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita continues singing at a newsstand about having nothing to lose and everything to gain.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHIHUAHUA DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: An SUV speeds through the Chihuahua Desert at night with narco-corrido music playing.

scenes.heading: INT. SUV - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Hooded Rita is violently jolted in the SUV and catches glimpses of the men and landscape through her hood.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The SUV passes a ruined bridge with corpses hanging from the railings.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The SUV joins a convoy of thirty armed trucks in the desert.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Still hooded, Rita is escorted through the armed convoy to Manitas' trailer where she has her first conversation with him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita's first meeting with Manitas establishes the central relationship and mission of the story

scenes.heading: INT. BERLINGER’S OFFICES - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Manitas tasks Rita with finding a surgeon for his gender transition surgery, offering unlimited resources.
scenes.turningPointNote: The central mission of the story is revealed - Manitas wants gender reassignment surgery

scenes.heading: EXT. SUVARNABHUMI AIRPORT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita meets with Asian surgeons in Bangkok to learn about gender reassignment procedures and techniques.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEN GURION AIRPORT, TEL AVIV – DAY
scenes.summary: An El-Al flight lands at Ben Gurion Airport in Tel Aviv.

scenes.heading: EXT. AVENUE, TEL AVIV - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita arrives at Hichilov Hospital in Tel Aviv.

scenes.heading: INT. LEVITCH MEDICAL CENTER, WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita waits in a medical center waiting room observing injured patients.

scenes.heading: INT. LEVITCH MEDICAL CENTER, WASSERMAN’S OFFICE – DAY
scenes.summary: Rita meets Dr. Wasserman and discusses her anonymous client's needs for surgery.

scenes.heading: INT. LEVITCH MEDICAL CENTER, WASSERMAN’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Wasserman asks to speak with Rita's client, and Rita warns that listening means accepting.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHIHUAHUA DESERT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wasserman travels with Rita to the Mexican desert, complaining about the loud music.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANITAS’ COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Armed men set up a temporary camp with projectors and canvas while Wasserman observes the cartel operation.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANITAS’ COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita introduces Manitas and Dr. Wasserman, who immediately agree to begin the consultation.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANITAS’ COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cartel camp becomes a party with music, dancing, women, and alcohol while Rita observes.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita meets Jessi Del Monte, Manitas' young wife, who questions what Rita does for her husband.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMPOUND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The party ends and sicarios sleep off their drinking in pickup trucks.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAMPEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wasserman gives Rita his notebook and explains the surgery will take place in Tel Aviv.

scenes.heading: INT. MANITAS’ TRAILER
scenes.summary: Rita gives Manitas new passports and financial accounts while he looks at his children's photos.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUSANNE VILLA
scenes.summary: The moon hangs over a Swiss villa by Lake Geneva.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA LAUSANNE
scenes.summary: Men move luggage and sleeping children into a Swiss villa while Jessi cries about being forced to leave Mexico.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita tells Manitas over the phone that his family is safe, and he declares she's his last link to the past.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wasserman's medical notebook is burned to ashes.

scenes.heading: INT. JESSI’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A news anchor reports that DNA evidence confirms Manitas Del Monte is dead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manitas' fake death is publicly confirmed, completing his transformation plan

scenes.heading: INT. JESSI’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jessi watches the news report about Manitas' death and cries out in anguish.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEL AVIV - DAY
scenes.summary: The sun rises over Tel Aviv.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM HICHILOV HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: A patient wakes up in a hospital room and sees Wasserman approaching.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM HICHILOV HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia examines her post-surgery body with a mirror and thanks God with delight.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manitas has successfully become Emilia, completing the physical transformation

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM HICHILOV HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia practices saying her new name while trying on bras in her hospital room.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Four years later, Rita dines at a chic London restaurant as a successful executive.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita meets a Mexican woman at the restaurant who reveals she's also from Mexico and has been living away.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita unknowingly encounters Emilia for the first time since the transformation

scenes.heading: INT. LAUSANNE VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jessi angrily refuses a phone request to return to Mexico, saying no one cared about her for four years.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUSANNE VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita and Jessi pack to return to Mexico, with Rita explaining they'll live with Emilia Pérez.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO CITY - DAY
scenes.summary: An aerial view shows Mexico City's sprawling urban landscape.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA'S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Voices announce the family's arrival at Emilia's luxurious hillside villa.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA'S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia greets Jessi as family, claiming to be like her sister and welcoming her home.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia reunites with her former wife under her new identity, a major emotional turning point

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita and Emilia quietly discuss Jessi's lost state and how Emilia must care for her.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: The sun rises over Emilia's villa with sprinklers watering the grounds.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia tries to connect with her children at breakfast, but they remain distant and suspicious.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: A maid carries a tray upstairs and walks down the corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA JESSI’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Jessi wakes in her room and sings 'Bienvenida' about being welcomed to a luxury prison, while dark ghostly silhouettes representing her troubled thoughts dance around her.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita and Emilia visit a market where Rita reconnects with the taqueria owner, then Emilia offers to pay Rita to stay and do nothing.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Emilia arrives home exhausted and drops a photo of a young disappeared person on a table.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Emilia checks on Ángel who tells her she smells like his father, leading to an emotional duet where the boy remembers his papa.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL PRISON - DAY
scenes.summary: A chaplain arrives at the central prison and gets into Emilia's car for a muted discussion.

scenes.heading: INT. CENTRAL PRISON/CONFESSIONAL - DAY
scenes.summary: In the prison confessional, sicario El Flaco confesses to the chaplain about killing hostages, possibly including the young man in the photo.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRISON, PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: The priest leaves the prison and speaks with Emilia through the limousine window.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED REFINERY, CIUDAD JUAREZ - DAY
scenes.summary: Ten men dig at an abandoned refinery in Ciudad Juarez, uncovering human remains including clothes, limbs, and skulls.

scenes.heading: INT. HANGAR - DAY
scenes.summary: In a hangar with ten body bags, Emilia embraces a grieving woman from the cemetery and sings 'La Lucecita' about feeling love for herself for the first time when the woman kissed her hands.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia experiences self-love and purpose through helping families of the disappeared, marking her transformation from criminal to humanitarian

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia and Rita give a press conference announcing La Lucecita NGO, with reporters asking about funding and methods.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA HEADQUARTERS - DAY
scenes.summary: In their empty headquarters, Rita worries about media scrutiny while Emilia dismissively suggests buying off reporters, revealing her continued criminal mindset.
scenes.turningPointNote: The tension between Emilia's criminal past and humanitarian present creates conflict with Rita, showing the impossibility of fully escaping one's history

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA HEADQUARTERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Families of the disappeared sing 'Para' in La Lucecita's waiting room, expressing their need to know what happened to their loved ones.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA’S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Emilia dances alone in her villa at night, visible through the bay windows.

scenes.heading: EXT. VILLA EMILIA - NUIT
scenes.summary: Jessi returns home at night, kissing someone in the car before walking barefoot up the gravel path, smiling.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA'S VILLA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Emilia questions an intoxicated Jessi about her marriage to Manitas, with Jessi revealing their relationship changed after having children.

scenes.heading: INT. FUNDRAISER GALA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a fundraising gala, reporters question Emilia about using sicarios to find remains while she deflects with humor.

scenes.heading: INT. FUNDRAISING GALA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: During Emilia's speech at the gala, Rita sings 'El Mal' unheard by the audience, revealing the criminal histories of the attending politicians and officials.

scenes.heading: INT. LA LUCECITA BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia arrives at La Lucecita offices in the morning, responding distractedly to greetings.

scenes.heading: INT. LA LUCECITA TOILETS - DAY
scenes.summary: A woman in the bathroom removes a knife from its sheath and puts it back in her bag.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The woman returns to her seat in the waiting room.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA EMILIA’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Epifanía meets with Emilia about her husband's remains, sarcastically asking if Emilia is sure he's really dead.

scenes.heading: INT. LA LUCECITA CORRIDORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia and Epifanía part ways in the corridor, with Emilia calling back to ask what to do with the body.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA CORRIDORS - DAY
scenes.summary: Epifanía tells Emilia to throw away her husband's corpse, and both women reveal they're armed with knife and gun respectively before Emilia asks to see her again.

scenes.heading: INT. EPIFANÍA’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia makes coffee in Epifanía's tiny house, feeling at home for the first time, before leaving to return home before the children wake up.

scenes.heading: INT. EPIFANÍA’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia sings 'El Amor' about being half everything and feeling love for the first time while Epifanía goes about her morning routine.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia experiences genuine romantic love for the first time, representing her emotional transformation and capacity for authentic human connection

scenes.heading: INT. LA LUCECITA OFFICES - DAY
scenes.summary: In their office, Rita admires Emilia for changing people's lives, contrasting it with her own past of helping rich people get richer.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita walks alone down a busy street and enters a nightclub.

scenes.heading: INT. NIGHTCLUB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the nightclub, Rita flirts with a man at the bar before discovering Jessi in another room with her new boyfriend Gustavo Brun and his friends.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia has breakfast with the children when she sees Jessi returning from her night out, silently warning her to be discreet around the children.

scenes.heading: EXT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia sends the children to school then goes upstairs to confront Jessi in her room.

scenes.heading: INT. VILLA JESSI’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia warns Jessi to be careful around the children, but Jessi suddenly announces she's getting married to Gustavo.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two men beat up Gustavo under a car, then give him money and threaten him to leave the city and stay away.

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S VILLA - DAY
scenes.summary: Emilia discovers Jessi has left with the children, taking all their belongings, and the three women sing a trio expressing their different perspectives on the situation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jessi's departure with the children devastates Emilia and sets up the final crisis, as Emilia loses the family that gave her new identity meaning

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA OFFICES - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rita and Epifanía wait together at La Lucecita offices, trying to reach Emilia who hasn't shown up for appointments.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO CITY STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: A young boy on a bicycle weaves through traffic carrying a package.

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: The boy delivers a wrapped package to Rita at La Lucecita before quickly leaving.

scenes.heading: INT. RITA’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita unwraps the blood-soaked package to find five severed fingers with Emilia's nail polish, then receives a threatening phone call from Gustavo.
scenes.turningPointNote: The discovery of Emilia's severed fingers reveals she has been captured and tortured, escalating the stakes to life and death

scenes.heading: INT. LUCECITA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita meets with El Ponchis and his armed men in the empty La Lucecita building, planning what appears to be a rescue operation.

scenes.heading: INT. RITA’S CAR - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rita drives expressionless with bags and a crackling walkie-talkie.

scenes.heading: EXT. RITA’S CAR + COUNTRY ROAD - EVENING
scenes.summary: Rita's car continues straight while three pickup trucks with armed men follow, then the convoy splits with Rita going left and the others right.

scenes.heading: INT. GUSTAVO’S CABIN, KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In Gustavo's cabin, a bloodied and bound Emilia asks Jessi for water and what they plan to do with her, with Jessi coldly responding they want their money back.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDSCAPE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: La Ponchis and her men take position behind rocks overlooking the cabin as Rita arrives below and arms herself.

scenes.heading: INT. GUSTAVO’S CABIN BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gustavo puts on night vision goggles and shouts orders to turn off lights as he watches Rita approach through the equipment.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDSCAPE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two shots are fired and Rita throws herself to the ground as La Ponchis gives her strategic hand signals.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUSTAVO’S CABIN + LANDSCAPE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Through his night vision, Gustavo orders Rita to advance and show her left hand while positioning his men for the exchange.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDSCAPE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita takes cover behind her pickup and watches smoke rise from the cabin then mysteriously flow back into the house.

scenes.heading: INT. HACIENDA, KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The smoke enters Emilia's mouth and she awakens, then reveals to Jessi that she is actually Manitas, Jessi's dead husband, confessing her love and transformation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia reveals her true identity as Manitas to Jessi in what appears to be her final moments, the ultimate truth that recontextualizes the entire story

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. GUSTAVO’S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Emilia is dragged into Gustavo's car trunk and makes eye contact with Jessi before the trunk slams shut, with Jessi realizing what they've done.

scenes.heading: INT. GUSTAVO BRUN’S CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the car, Jessi tells Gustavo that Emilia is actually Manitas, her husband, and grabs the wheel trying to force him to stop.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUSTAVO BRUN’S CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A car zigzags while being shot at with detonations and muzzle flashes illuminating the interior, then swerves and flies down an embankment.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUSTAVO BRUN’S CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The crashed car sits at the bottom of the ravine a hundred meters below.

scenes.heading: EXT. GUSTAVO BRUN’S CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The camera approaches the car's trunk as mechanical sounds emerge from the wreckage, then the car suddenly explodes in flames.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOUNTAIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rita watches powerlessly from above as the car burns as an inferno in the ravine below, red flames reflecting on her face.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rita witnesses what appears to be the final destruction of her connection to the cartel world

scenes.heading: INT. EMILIA’S HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rita, dressed in black and defeated, watches from a window as Diego and Ángel are escorted by police while calling them her children and family.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICO CITY SUBURBS - DAY
scenes.summary: Epifanía leads a funeral procession singing about someone who changed lead to gold and raised the flag of truth, while mourners carry a naive statue of Emilia.
scenes.turningPointNote: Emilia is memorialized and elevated to folk saint status by the community she helped
